en.mythology: Avior is part of Carina, the Keel, which was historically part of the vast ancient constellation Argo Navis — the ship of Jason and the Argonauts. In Greek mythology, the Argo was the legendary vessel built by the craftsman Argus under the guidance of Athena, used by Jason and his fifty heroic companions in their quest for the Golden Fleece. The ship was said to have been built with a plank from the sacred oak grove of Dodona, giving it the ability to speak and prophesy. After the successful quest, the gods honored the Argo by placing it in the sky. In 1930, the International Astronomical Union divided the unwieldy Argo Navis into three separate constellations: Carina (the keel), Puppis (the stern), and Vela (the sails).

id.description: Avior adalah salah satu bintang paling terang di konstelasi selatan Carina (Lunas), terletak sekitar 630 tahun cahaya dari Bumi. Ini adalah sistem bintang majemuk — komponen utama adalah raksasa oranye yang dingin bertipe K3III, sementara pendampingnya adalah bintang tipe B biru-putih yang panas. Ini menjadikan Avior sistem biner dengan warna yang kontras. Avior tidak memiliki nama tradisional dalam daftar bintang Arab atau Yunani kuno — namanya saat ini ditetapkan oleh British Nautical Almanac Office pada abad ke-20 untuk keperluan navigasi.

Dalam mitologi Yunani, Argo adalah kapal legendaris yang dibangun oleh pengrajin Argus di bawah bimbingan Athena, digunakan oleh Jason dan lima puluh pahlawan dalam pencarian Bulu Domba Emas. Pada 1930, IAU membagi Argo Navis yang besar menjadi tiga konstelasi terpisah: Carina (lunas), Puppis (buritan), dan Vela (layar).

en.description: Pollux is the brightest star in Gemini and the seventeenth-brightest star in the night sky, located about 34 light-years from Earth. It is an orange giant approximately 9 times the diameter of the Sun and 32 times more luminous. Pollux is notable for hosting a confirmed exoplanet — Pollux b (also called Thestias), one of the first exoplanets discovered around a giant star. This Jupiter-sized planet orbits Pollux every 590 days. Pollux is slightly brighter than its mythological twin Castor despite being designated Beta Geminorum.

en.mythology: Pollux was named after one of the Divine Twins (Dioscuri) of Greek and Roman mythology. Castor and Pollux were the twin sons of Leda — but with different fathers. Castor was the mortal son of King Tyndareus of Sparta, while Pollux was the immortal son of Zeus. The two were inseparable and together became famous as patrons of sailors and fighters. When Castor died in battle, the immortal Pollux was so grief-stricken that he asked Zeus to share his immortality with his brother. Zeus placed them together in the sky as the constellation Gemini. In Roman tradition, Castor and Pollux were credited with appearing as balls of light (St. Elmo's Fire) on the masts of ships during storms to protect sailors.

Planet seukuran Jupiter ini mengorbit Pollux setiap 590 hari.

Castor adalah putra manusia fana Raja Tyndareus dari Sparta, sementara Pollux adalah putra abadi Zeus. Keduanya tidak terpisahkan dan bersama-sama menjadi pelindung para pelaut dan pejuang. Ketika Castor tewas dalam pertempuran, Pollux yang abadi meminta Zeus untuk berbagi keabadiannya dengan saudaranya. Zeus menempatkan mereka bersama di langit sebagai konstelasi Gemini.

en.description: Castor is the second-brightest star in Gemini and one of the most remarkable star systems in the sky — what appears as a single star to the naked eye is actually a sextuple star system, consisting of three binary pairs orbiting a common center of mass. Located 51 light-years away, the six stars include two blue-white A-type stars, two fainter F-type stars, and a pair of red dwarf stars. Through a small telescope, Castor appears as a beautiful close double star. Despite being labeled Alpha Geminorum, Castor is actually slightly fainter than its twin Pollux.

en.mythology: Castor was named after one of the Divine Twins (Dioscuri) of Greek and Roman mythology — the mortal son of King Tyndareus of Sparta, while his twin Pollux was the immortal son of Zeus. Together they were known as patrons of sailors and navigators, appearing as St. Elmo's Fire to protect ships in storms. When Castor died in battle, Pollux was devastated and begged Zeus to share his immortality. Zeus placed both brothers in the sky as the constellation Gemini, where they would forever travel together across the heavens. The Dioscuri were also celebrated as skilled horsemen and warriors — Castor was particularly famed as a horse tamer, and their symbol, twin stars, appeared on the shields of Spartan warriors.

en.description: Regulus is the brightest star in Leo and the twenty-first brightest star in the night sky, located about 79 light-years from Earth. It marks the heart of the lion. Regulus is one of the four 'royal stars' of ancient Persian astronomy. It is actually a quadruple star system. Regulus A is a blue-white main-sequence star rotating so rapidly — once every 15.9 hours — that it bulges noticeably at its equator. If it spun just 16% faster, it would fly apart. Regulus lies almost exactly on the ecliptic — the apparent path of the Sun across the sky — meaning it is one of the few bright stars that can be occulted by the Moon, planets, and asteroids.

en.mythology: Regulus means 'little king' in Latin, and this star has been associated with royalty across many cultures. In ancient Persia it was one of the four 'royal stars' — Regulus (spring), Aldebaran (summer), Antares (autumn), and Fomalhaut (winter) — believed to guard the four quarters of the sky and linked to the four archangels. In Greek mythology, Leo represented the Nemean Lion, the first of Hercules's twelve labors. The lion was a monstrous beast with an impenetrable golden hide that no weapon could pierce. Hercules had to strangle it with his bare hands, then used the lion's own claws to skin it. Zeus immortalized the beast in the sky as Leo. In ancient Egypt, Regulus rising with the Sun announced the annual flooding of the Nile.

id.description: Regulus adalah bintang paling terang di Leo dan bintang paling terang ke-21 di langit malam, terletak sekitar 79 tahun cahaya dari Bumi. Ini menandai jantung singa. Regulus adalah salah satu dari empat 'bintang kerajaan' astronomi Persia kuno. Ini sebenarnya adalah sistem bintang empat. Regulus A adalah bintang deret utama biru-putih yang berputar sangat cepat — sekali setiap 15,9 jam — sehingga menggelembung terlihat di ekuatornya. Regulus terletak hampir tepat di ekliptika — lintasan semu Matahari melintasi langit.

id.mythology: Regulus berarti 'raja kecil' dalam bahasa Latin, dan bintang ini telah dikaitkan dengan kerajaan di banyak budaya. Dalam Persia kuno ia adalah salah satu dari empat 'bintang kerajaan' yang diyakini menjaga empat penjuru langit. Dalam mitologi Yunani, Leo mewakili Singa Nemean, tugas pertama dari dua belas tugas Hercules. Singa itu adalah makhluk mengerikan dengan kulit emas yang tidak dapat ditembus senjata apapun. Hercules harus mencekiknya dengan tangan kosong, lalu menggunakan cakar singa itu sendiri untuk menguliti. Zeus mengabadikan binatang itu di langit sebagai Leo.

en.description: Denebola is the second-brightest star in Leo, marking the tail of the lion, located about 36 light-years from Earth. It is a young, hot white main-sequence star about 1.75 times the mass of the Sun and 15 times more luminous. Denebola is notable for having an infrared excess — indicating a debris disk of warm dust surrounding it, suggesting the possibility of planets or proto-planetary material. It rotates rapidly at about 128 km/s and shows slight variability in brightness. Denebola forms part of the Spring Triangle asterism with Arcturus and Spica.

en.mythology: Denebola's Arabic name 'dhanab al-asad' means 'the lion's tail', placing it at the eastern end of the constellation Leo. In Greek mythology, Leo represented the Nemean Lion, the first of the twelve labors of Hercules. After strangling the invulnerable beast and skinning it with its own claws, Hercules wore the lion's impenetrable hide as armor for the rest of his adventures. Zeus honored the lion by placing it in the sky. In medieval astrology, Denebola was considered one of the 15 Behenian stars with particular magical properties, associated with misfortune and disgrace when in certain positions — a reputation that made medieval astrologers wary of its influence despite its brightness.

id.description: Denebola adalah bintang paling terang kedua di Leo, menandai ekor singa, terletak sekitar 36 tahun cahaya dari Bumi. Denebola dikenal memiliki kelebihan inframerah — menunjukkan cakram puing debu hangat di sekelilingnya, menunjukkan kemungkinan adanya planet. Denebola membentuk bagian dari asterisma Segitiga Musim Semi bersama Arcturus dan Spica.

Setelah mencekik binatang yang tidak bisa ditembus dan menguliti dengan cakarnya sendiri, Hercules mengenakan kulit singa yang tidak bisa ditembus sebagai baju besi. Zeus menghormati singa dengan menempatkannya di langit.

en.description: Algieba is one of the finest double stars in the sky, located in Leo about 130 light-years from Earth. Through a telescope, it resolves into two beautiful golden-orange giant stars orbiting each other — one of the most visually stunning binary star systems for amateur astronomers. The primary star Algieba A is about 180 times more luminous than the Sun, while Algieba B is about 50 times more luminous. The two stars take approximately 510 years to complete one orbit around each other. Algieba marks the mane or neck of Leo the Lion.

en.mythology: Algieba marks the mane of Leo the Lion, the great celestial lion of Greek mythology. Leo was placed in the sky by Zeus to honor the Nemean Lion, the fearsome beast with invulnerable golden fur that was the first of Hercules's twelve labors. Unable to harm the lion with any weapon, Hercules wrestled it to the ground and choked it with his bare hands, using its own claws to skin it. He wore the lion's pelt as armor throughout his subsequent adventures, making him nearly invincible. In ancient Babylonian astronomy, Leo was one of the most important constellations — the lion was the symbol of kingship and divine power, and the sun's passage through Leo during summer was associated with the height of royal power.

en.description: Spica is the brightest star in Virgo and the fifteenth-brightest star in the night sky, located about 250 light-years from Earth. It is a binary system of two stars so close together they cannot be separated visually — they orbit each other every 4 days. Both stars are hot blue-white giants. The primary is about 10 times the mass of the Sun and 12,100 times more luminous. Spica is one of the nearest massive binary stars to Earth. It is historically significant — the ancient Greek astronomer Hipparchus used Spica to discover the precession of the equinoxes around 127 BCE by comparing his observations with earlier records.

en.mythology: Spica represents the ear of wheat or grain held in the left hand of Virgo the Maiden in the classical depiction of the constellation. Virgo was associated with Demeter (Greek) or Ceres (Roman), the goddess of grain and agriculture. In the myth, Demeter's daughter Persephone was abducted by Hades and taken to the underworld. Demeter's grief caused the earth to become barren — the origin of winter. When Persephone was returned for part of the year, the earth flourished — the origin of spring and summer. Spica, as the grain Demeter holds, embodies her role as the earth mother and nourisher of humanity. The star was used by Hipparchus to make one of astronomy's most significant discoveries, and was later used by Nicolaus Copernicus in developing his heliocentric model.

id.description: Spica adalah bintang paling terang di Virgo dan bintang paling terang ke-15 di langit malam, terletak sekitar 250 tahun cahaya dari Bumi. Kedua bintang adalah raksasa biru-putih panas. Spica secara historis penting — astronom Yunani kuno Hipparchus menggunakan Spica untuk menemukan presesi ekuinoks sekitar 127 SM.

Dalam mitos, putri Demeter, Persephone, diculik oleh Hades dan dibawa ke dunia bawah. Kesedihan Demeter menyebabkan bumi menjadi tandus — asal mula musim dingin. Ketika Persephone dikembalikan selama sebagian tahun, bumi kembali subur — asal mula musim semi dan panas. Spica, sebagai gandum yang dipegang Demeter, melambangkan perannya sebagai ibu bumi dan pemelihara umat manusia.

en.description: Zubeneschamali is the brightest star in Libra, marking the northern scale pan of the celestial balance, located about 185 light-years from Earth. It is a blue-white main-sequence star about 3.5 times the mass of the Sun. Zubeneschamali is notable for being occasionally reported as having a greenish tint — one of the very few stars that some observers describe as looking green to the naked eye, though this remains debated among astronomers. Its name comes from Arabic meaning 'the northern claw', a remnant of when Libra's stars were considered part of Scorpius's claws.

en.mythology: Zubeneschamali's Arabic name 'al-zuban al-shamaliyy' means 'the northern claw', revealing its ancient history as part of Scorpius rather than Libra. In Babylonian astronomy, this region of the sky was the claws of the scorpion. The ancient Romans transformed the scorpion's claws into a separate constellation — Libra, the Scales — to honor Julius Caesar and the concept of justice and balance. Libra was associated with Astraea, the goddess of justice, who held the scales to weigh the souls of the dead. It was the constellation of the autumnal equinox in ancient times, when day and night were balanced — reinforcing the scales symbolism. Libra remains the only zodiac constellation representing an inanimate object.

id.description: Zubeneschamali adalah bintang paling terang di Libra, menandai piringan timbangan utara dari neraca langit, terletak sekitar 185 tahun cahaya dari Bumi. Ini adalah bintang deret utama biru-putih sekitar 3,5 kali massa Matahari. Zubeneschamali dikenal karena kadang dilaporkan memiliki warna kehijauan — salah satu dari sangat sedikit bintang yang beberapa pengamat gambarkan terlihat hijau bagi mata telanjang. Namanya berasal dari bahasa Arab yang berarti 'cakar utara', sisa dari masa ketika bintang-bintang Libra dianggap sebagai bagian dari cakar Scorpius.

id.mythology: Nama Arab Zubeneschamali 'al-zuban al-shamaliyy' berarti 'cakar utara', mengungkap sejarah kunonya sebagai bagian dari Scorpius. Dalam astronomi Babilonia, wilayah langit ini adalah cakar kalajengking. Orang Romawi kuno mengubah cakar kalajengking menjadi konstelasi terpisah — Libra, Timbangan — untuk menghormati Julius Caesar dan konsep keadilan. Libra dikaitkan dengan Astraea, dewi keadilan, yang memegang timbangan untuk menimbang jiwa orang mati. Libra tetap menjadi satu-satunya konstelasi zodiak yang mewakili benda mati.

en.description: Zubenelgenubi is the second-brightest star in Libra, marking the southern scale pan of the celestial balance, located about 77 light-years from Earth. It is a naked-eye double star — two stars separated enough to be resolved without optical aid under good conditions, making it one of the few naked-eye doubles in the sky. The primary component is an A-type main-sequence star, while the secondary is a fainter F-type star. Its Arabic name means 'the southern claw', another remnant of its ancient history as part of Scorpius.

en.mythology: Zubenelgenubi's Arabic name 'al-zuban al-janubiyy' means 'the southern claw', like its northern counterpart Zubeneschamali, betraying its ancient origin as part of Scorpius in Babylonian and early Greek astronomy. The Romans separated the scorpion's claws into Libra to create a constellation honoring justice — associated with the goddess Astraea, the last immortal to leave Earth when humans became corrupt, who carried the scales of divine justice. In some traditions, Libra was held by the goddess Themis or Dike. The autumn equinox, when day and night are balanced, once occurred when the sun was in Libra — making the scales an especially apt symbol for this region of the sky.

id.description: Zubenelgenubi adalah bintang paling terang kedua di Libra, menandai piringan timbangan selatan dari neraca langit, terletak sekitar 77 tahun cahaya dari Bumi. Ini adalah bintang ganda yang terlihat mata telanjang — dua bintang yang cukup terpisah untuk diselesaikan tanpa alat optik dalam kondisi baik. Nama Arabnya berarti 'cakar selatan', sisa dari sejarah kunonya sebagai bagian dari Scorpius.

Orang Romawi memisahkan cakar kalajengking menjadi Libra untuk menciptakan konstelasi yang menghormati keadilan — dikaitkan dengan dewi Astraea, makhluk abadi terakhir yang meninggalkan Bumi ketika manusia menjadi korup, yang membawa timbangan keadilan ilahi.

en.description: Kaus Australis is the brightest star in Sagittarius, marking the southern part of the archer's bow, located approximately 143 light-years from Earth. It is a blue-white giant of spectral type B9.5III. The name 'Kaus Australis' combines the Arabic word for bow ('qaws') with the Latin word for southern ('australis'). Sagittarius points toward the center of our galaxy, and the region around this star is one of the richest star fields in the sky, packed with star clusters and nebulae including the Lagoon Nebula and Trifid Nebula.

en.mythology: Kaus Australis is part of Sagittarius the Archer, one of the most complex constellations in mythology. In Greek tradition, Sagittarius represented the centaur Chiron or the satyr Crotus, son of Pan, who invented the art of archery. Crotus lived on Mount Helicon among the Muses and invented the practice of clapping to show appreciation — the Muses asked Zeus to honor him with a place in the sky. The 'Teapot' asterism of Sagittarius, which includes Kaus Australis, points directly toward the center of the Milky Way, which ancient cultures across the world recognized as a particularly powerful region of the sky. In Babylonian astronomy, Sagittarius was a two-faced god, depicted as a centaur-like figure with both a human face and a scorpion's face.

id.description: Kaus Australis adalah bintang paling terang di Sagittarius, menandai bagian selatan busur pemanah, terletak sekitar 143 tahun cahaya dari Bumi. Ini adalah raksasa biru-putih bertipe spektral B9.5III. Nama 'Kaus Australis' menggabungkan kata Arab untuk busur ('qaws') dengan kata Latin untuk selatan ('australis'). Sagittarius mengarah ke pusat galaksi kita, dan wilayah sekitar bintang ini adalah salah satu medan bintang terkaya di langit.

id.mythology: Kaus Australis adalah bagian dari Sagittarius sang Pemanah. Dalam tradisi Yunani, Sagittarius mewakili centaur Chiron atau satyr Crotus, putra Pan, yang menemukan seni memanah. Crotus tinggal di Gunung Helicon bersama para Muse dan menemukan kebiasaan bertepuk tangan sebagai penghargaan — para Muse meminta Zeus untuk menghormatinya dengan tempat di langit. Asterisma 'Teapot' Sagittarius mengarah langsung ke pusat Bima Sakti.

en.description: Nunki is the second-brightest star in Sagittarius and one of the oldest recorded star names in history, dating back to ancient Babylonian astronomy. Located approximately 228 light-years from Earth, it is a hot blue-white main-sequence star about 5 times the mass of the Sun and 3,300 times more luminous. The name Nunki is of Sumerian origin, believed to refer to a sacred Babylonian city or to the sea. Nunki is part of the 'Teapot' asterism of Sagittarius and lies in a region pointing toward the center of our galaxy.

en.mythology: Nunki holds the distinction of having one of the oldest recorded star names in human history, traced to ancient Sumerian and Babylonian astronomy dating back thousands of years. In Babylonian star catalogs, Nunki was associated with the god Enki (also called Ea), the god of wisdom, water, intelligence, and creation who lived in the abzu — the underground freshwater ocean beneath the earth. The name may derive from 'mul.NUN.KI' — the Babylonian name for the city of Eridu, considered the oldest city in the world and the first city founded by the gods. Sagittarius as a whole was seen by the Babylonians as a two-headed god, and this region of the sky near the galactic center was associated with profound cosmic significance.

id.description: Nunki adalah bintang paling terang kedua di Sagittarius dan salah satu nama bintang tertua yang tercatat dalam sejarah, berasal dari astronomi Babilonia kuno. Terletak sekitar 228 tahun cahaya dari Bumi, ini adalah bintang deret utama biru-putih panas sekitar 5 kali massa Matahari dan 3.300 kali lebih terang. Nama Nunki berasal dari bahasa Sumeria, diyakini merujuk pada kota suci Babilonia.

id.mythology: Nunki memiliki keistimewaan sebagai salah satu nama bintang tertua yang tercatat dalam sejarah manusia, dapat ditelusuri ke astronomi Sumeria dan Babilonia kuno ribuan tahun yang lalu. Dalam katalog bintang Babilonia, Nunki dikaitkan dengan dewa Enki (juga disebut Ea), dewa kebijaksanaan, air, kecerdasan, dan penciptaan yang tinggal di abzu — lautan air tawar bawah tanah di bawah bumi. Namanya mungkin berasal dari 'mul.NUN.KI' — nama Babilonia untuk kota Eridu, dianggap sebagai kota tertua di dunia.

en.description: Ascella is the third-brightest star in Sagittarius, marking the armpit of the archer, located about 89 light-years from Earth. It is a close binary star system — two white A-type stars orbiting each other so closely that they cannot be separated visually. The combined system is about 2.5 times the mass of the Sun and 25 times more luminous. Ascella's name comes directly from Latin meaning 'armpit' or 'axilla', describing its anatomical position in the constellation figure of the archer. Despite its modest appearance, it lies relatively close to Earth compared to other Sagittarius stars.

en.mythology: Ascella is part of Sagittarius the Archer, whose mythological identity in Greek tradition has two competing versions. In one version, Sagittarius represents Crotus, son of the goat-god Pan and a nymph, who was a skilled hunter and musician who lived among the Muses on Mount Helicon. He invented archery and the practice of rhythmic clapping. In another version, it represents the wise centaur Chiron, tutor of heroes including Achilles, Jason, and Asclepius. In Babylonian astronomy, this constellation was depicted as a deity with a human torso, horse body, and scorpion tail — a complex figure associated with the god Pabilsag, ruler of the underworld and war.

id.description: Ascella adalah bintang paling terang ketiga di Sagittarius, menandai ketiak pemanah, terletak sekitar 89 tahun cahaya dari Bumi. Ini adalah sistem bintang biner yang rapat — dua bintang tipe A putih yang mengorbit satu sama lain begitu dekat sehingga tidak dapat dipisahkan secara visual. Nama Ascella berasal langsung dari bahasa Latin yang berarti 'ketiak' atau 'axilla', menggambarkan posisi anatomisnya dalam tokoh konstelasi pemanah.

Dalam satu versi, Sagittarius mewakili Crotus, putra dewa kambing Pan, yang merupakan pemburu dan musisi terampil yang tinggal di antara para Muse. Dalam versi lain, ia mewakili centaur bijak Chiron, pengajar para pahlawan termasuk Achilles dan Jason.

en.description: Deneb Algedi is the brightest star in Capricornus, the Sea-Goat, located about 39 light-years from Earth — making it one of the closest bright stars in this constellation. It is an eclipsing binary system where a larger star is periodically dimmed by a smaller companion passing in front of it, with a period of 24.5 hours. The primary star is a white giant of spectral type A7III. Its name means 'the tail of the goat' in Arabic. Despite being in a faint and unremarkable constellation, Deneb Algedi's proximity makes it relatively easy to study.

en.mythology: Deneb Algedi marks the tail of Capricornus, one of the oldest constellations in human history — its origins trace back at least 3,000 years to ancient Babylonian astronomy. In Greek mythology, Capricornus represents the god Pan, who transformed himself into a sea-goat to escape the monster Typhon. Pan jumped into the Nile River but only partially transformed — his lower body became a fish tail while his upper body remained a goat, creating the distinctive chimeric figure. Zeus placed the image in the sky to commemorate the escape. In Babylonian astronomy, the constellation was known as 'SUHUR.MAS' — the goat-fish — associated with the god Enki/Ea, who was often depicted with a goat's head and a fish's tail, embodying the connection between land and water.

id.description: Deneb Algedi adalah bintang paling terang di Capricornus, Kambing Laut, terletak sekitar 39 tahun cahaya dari Bumi — menjadikannya salah satu bintang terang terdekat di konstelasi ini. Ini adalah sistem biner gerhana di mana bintang yang lebih besar secara periodik diredup oleh pendamping yang lebih kecil yang melintas di depannya, dengan periode 24,5 jam. Namanya berarti 'ekor kambing' dalam bahasa Arab.

id.mythology: Deneb Algedi menandai ekor Capricornus, salah satu konstelasi tertua dalam sejarah manusia — asalnya dapat ditelusuri setidaknya 3.000 tahun ke astronomi Babilonia kuno. Dalam mitologi Yunani, Capricornus mewakili dewa Pan, yang mengubah dirinya menjadi kambing laut untuk melarikan diri dari monster Typhon. Pan melompat ke Sungai Nil tetapi hanya berubah sebagian — tubuh bawahnya menjadi ekor ikan sementara tubuh atasnya tetap kambing. Zeus menempatkan gambaran itu di langit untuk memperingati pelariannya.

en.description: Dabih is a naked-eye double star in Capricornus, located approximately 344 light-years from Earth. The brighter component, Dabih Major (Beta1 Capricorni), is a multiple star system containing a yellow giant and a hot blue companion. The fainter Dabih Minor (Beta2 Capricorni) is a blue-white star that appears close in the sky but is actually much farther away at about 328 light-years. Despite appearing as one star to the naked eye, the two components are only loosely associated. Its name comes from Arabic meaning 'the butcher' — referring to ritual animal sacrifices associated with the star's heliacal rising in ancient times.

en.mythology: Dabih's Arabic name 'al-dabih' means 'the lucky stars of the slaughterer' or 'the butcher', referring to the practice of ritual animal sacrifice that ancient Arab and Babylonian cultures performed when the star rose with the sun in a particular season. In Babylonian astronomy, Capricornus (as the sea-goat SUHUR.MAS) was associated with the winter solstice and the transition between the old and new year — a time of sacrifice and renewal. In Greek mythology, this constellation marks the form Pan took when escaping the monster Typhon by leaping into the Nile, only partially transforming into a fish-goat hybrid. Zeus honored this transformation by placing the image in the sky.

id.description: Dabih adalah bintang ganda yang terlihat mata telanjang di Capricornus, terletak sekitar 344 tahun cahaya dari Bumi. Komponen yang lebih terang, Dabih Major, adalah sistem bintang majemuk yang mengandung raksasa kuning dan pendamping biru panas. Komponen yang lebih redup, Dabih Minor, adalah bintang biru-putih yang tampak dekat di langit tetapi sebenarnya jauh lebih jauh. Namanya berasal dari bahasa Arab yang berarti 'tukang jagal' — merujuk pada pengorbanan ritual hewan yang dikaitkan dengan terbitnya bintang ini di zaman kuno.

id.mythology: Nama Arab Dabih 'al-dabih' berarti 'bintang keberuntungan sang jagal', merujuk pada praktik pengorbanan ritual hewan yang dilakukan budaya Arab dan Babilonia kuno ketika bintang ini terbit bersama matahari pada musim tertentu. Dalam astronomi Babilonia, Capricornus dikaitkan dengan titik balik matahari musim dingin dan transisi antara tahun lama dan baru — waktu pengorbanan dan pembaruan.

en.description: Sadalsuud is the brightest star in Aquarius and one of the most luminous yellow supergiants visible to the naked eye, located about 540 light-years from Earth. Its name in Arabic means 'the luckiest of the lucky' — one of the most auspicious star names in the Arabic astronomical tradition. It is a yellow supergiant roughly 2,200 times more luminous than the Sun. Ancient Arab astronomers associated Sadalsuud with the arrival of spring rains, making it a 'lucky' star associated with the renewal of life. The star rises heliacally in spring, heralding the end of winter.

en.mythology: Sadalsuud's name 'sa'd al-su'ud' means 'the luckiest of the lucky' in Arabic, making it the most auspiciously named star in the Arabic astronomical tradition. Ancient Arab astronomers organized the sky into 28 'lunar mansions' (manzil al-qamar), and the stars of Aquarius were associated with particularly favorable lunar stations. Sadalsuud's heliacal rising coincided with the welcome arrival of spring rains in the arid Arabian peninsula — water being literally life. In Greek mythology, Aquarius represents Ganymede, the beautiful Trojan youth kidnapped by Zeus's eagle to serve as cupbearer to the gods on Olympus. In Babylonian astronomy, the constellation was associated with the god Ea (Enki), whose domain was fresh water, wisdom, and creation.

id.description: Sadalsuud adalah bintang paling terang di Aquarius dan salah satu supergiant kuning paling terang yang terlihat mata telanjang, terletak sekitar 540 tahun cahaya dari Bumi. Namanya dalam bahasa Arab berarti 'yang paling beruntung dari yang beruntung' — salah satu nama bintang paling bertuah dalam tradisi astronomi Arab. Ini adalah supergiant kuning sekitar 2.200 kali lebih terang dari Matahari.

id.mythology: Nama Sadalsuud 'sa'd al-su'ud' berarti 'yang paling beruntung dari yang beruntung' dalam bahasa Arab, menjadikannya bintang dengan nama paling bertuah dalam tradisi astronomi Arab. Astronom Arab kuno mengorganisir langit menjadi 28 'manzil al-qamar' (istana bulan), dan bintang-bintang Aquarius dikaitkan dengan stasiun lunar yang sangat menguntungkan. Dalam mitologi Yunani, Aquarius mewakili Ganymede, pemuda Troya tampan yang diculik elang Zeus untuk melayani para dewa di Olimpus.

en.description: Sadalmelik is the second-brightest star in Aquarius, marking the right shoulder of the water bearer, located about 520 light-years from Earth. It is a yellow supergiant of spectral type G2Ib, roughly 3,000 times more luminous than the Sun and about 77 times its radius. Its Arabic name means 'the lucky stars of the king', another of the auspiciously named stars in this constellation. Like Sadalsuud, its heliacal rising in ancient times signaled the onset of spring and the rainy season, making it a symbol of blessing and prosperity.

en.mythology: Sadalmelik's Arabic name 'sa'd al-malik' means 'the lucky stars of the king', sharing the auspicious 'sa'd' prefix with its neighbor Sadalsuud. In the ancient Arabic lunar mansion system, both stars formed part of the 24th and 25th mansions — stations associated with rainfall, good fortune, and the renewal of life after winter. In Greek mythology, the constellation Aquarius represents Ganymede, the most beautiful of mortal youths, whom Zeus abducted in the form of an eagle to serve as cupbearer (aquarius) to the gods on Olympus. Ganymede was immortalized by Zeus and became the divine cupbearer, pouring water and nectar for the gods — the Milky Way itself was sometimes described as the water he poured from his urn.

id.description: Sadalmelik adalah bintang paling terang kedua di Aquarius, menandai bahu kanan pembawa air, terletak sekitar 520 tahun cahaya dari Bumi. Ini adalah supergiant kuning bertipe spektral G2Ib, sekitar 3.000 kali lebih terang dari Matahari dan sekitar 77 kali radiusnya. Namanya dalam bahasa Arab berarti 'bintang keberuntungan raja', salah satu bintang dengan nama bertuah di konstelasi ini.

id.mythology: Nama Arab Sadalmelik 'sa'd al-malik' berarti 'bintang keberuntungan raja', berbagi awalan 'sa'd' yang bertuah dengan tetangganya Sadalsuud. Dalam sistem mansil bulan Arab kuno, kedua bintang ini membentuk bagian dari mansil ke-24 dan ke-25 — stasiun yang dikaitkan dengan hujan, keberuntungan, dan pembaruan kehidupan setelah musim dingin. Dalam mitologi Yunani, Aquarius mewakili Ganymede, pemuda fana paling tampan, yang diculik Zeus dalam wujud elang untuk melayani sebagai penuang air bagi para dewa di Olimpus.

en.description: Alrescha marks the knot where the cords of the two fish of Pisces are tied together, located about 139 light-years from Earth. It is a binary star system — two white A-type stars orbiting each other with a period of about 933 years. The primary is about 2 times the mass of the Sun. Alrescha lies near the vernal equinox — the point where the ecliptic crosses the celestial equator — making it historically significant as a reference point for the start of the zodiacal year. Its name comes from Arabic meaning 'the cord' or 'the rope', perfectly describing its role as the tie binding the two fish of Pisces.

en.mythology: Alrescha marks the knot in the cord that binds the two fish of Pisces together in Greek mythology. The constellation represents Aphrodite and her son Eros, who transformed into fish and tied themselves together with a rope to escape the monster Typhon, who had descended upon Mount Olympus terrorizing the gods. Zeus later immortalized their clever escape by placing the two fish in the sky. The binding cord, marked by Alrescha, symbolizes the unbreakable bond between mother and child. Pisces was also associated with the goddess Venus in Roman tradition, and in ancient Babylonian astronomy the two fish represented the tails of the goddess Anunitum and the swallow, both connected to the goddess Ishtar.

id.description: Alrescha menandai simpul di mana tali kedua ikan Pisces diikat bersama, terletak sekitar 139 tahun cahaya dari Bumi. Ini adalah sistem bintang biner — dua bintang tipe A putih yang mengorbit satu sama lain dengan periode sekitar 933 tahun. Alrescha terletak dekat titik vernal equinox, menjadikannya penting secara historis sebagai titik referensi untuk awal tahun zodiak. Namanya berasal dari bahasa Arab yang berarti 'tali' atau 'tambang'.

id.mythology: Alrescha menandai simpul di tali yang mengikat dua ikan Pisces bersama dalam mitologi Yunani. Konstelasi ini mewakili Aphrodite dan putranya Eros, yang berubah menjadi ikan dan mengikat diri bersama dengan tali untuk melarikan diri dari monster Typhon yang menyerang Olimpus. Zeus mengabadikan pelarian cerdas mereka dengan menempatkan dua ikan di langit. Tali pengikat, yang ditandai oleh Alrescha, melambangkan ikatan yang tak terputuskan antara ibu dan anak.

en.description: Fumalsamakah is the brightest star in Pisces, marking the mouth of the northern fish, located approximately 294 light-years from Earth. It is a cool orange giant of spectral type K5III, about 75 times the radius of the Sun and roughly 1,600 times more luminous. Its Arabic name means 'the fish's mouth', precisely describing its anatomical position in the constellation. Pisces is a faint constellation with no stars brighter than magnitude 3.5, making Fumalsamakah a relatively modest but important marker for the zodiacal constellation.

en.mythology: Fumalsamakah is part of Pisces, the twelfth and final sign of the zodiac. In Greek mythology, Pisces represents Aphrodite and her son Eros, who transformed into fish and tied themselves together with a cord to escape the monster Typhon, who had descended upon Mount Olympus terrorizing the gods. The two fish swim in opposite directions, tethered together by Alrescha (the cord). In Babylonian astronomy, the constellation was known as 'the tails' (mul.A.A) and depicted two fish with their tails tied together, associated with the goddess Anunitum (the northern fish) and the swallow (the southern fish), both connected to Ishtar. Pisces being the last zodiac sign was associated with endings, dissolution, and the transition to a new cycle — the fish returning to the primal waters before the new zodiac year began with Aries.

id.description: Fumalsamakah adalah bintang paling terang di Pisces, menandai mulut ikan utara, terletak sekitar 294 tahun cahaya dari Bumi. Ini adalah raksasa oranye yang dingin bertipe spektral K5III, sekitar 75 kali radius Matahari dan sekitar 1.600 kali lebih terang. Namanya dalam bahasa Arab berarti 'mulut ikan', menggambarkan dengan tepat posisi anatomisnya di konstelasi.

id.mythology: Fumalsamakah adalah bagian dari Pisces, tanda kedua belas dan terakhir dari zodiak. Dalam mitologi Yunani, Pisces mewakili Aphrodite dan putranya Eros, yang berubah menjadi ikan dan mengikat diri bersama dengan tali untuk melarikan diri dari monster Typhon. Dalam astronomi Babilonia, konstelasi dikenal sebagai 'ekor-ekor' (mul.A.A) dan digambarkan sebagai dua ikan dengan ekor yang diikat bersama, dikaitkan dengan dewi Anunitum dan burung layang-layang, keduanya terhubung dengan Ishtar. Pisces sebagai tanda zodiak terakhir dikaitkan dengan akhir, pembubaran, dan transisi ke siklus baru.
